title: Engineer, Software Quality
description:
Key Responsibilities:

  • Test Planning: Assist in the creation of test cases and test scenarios based on the product and software requirements.
  • Test Execution: Execute software tests with the device to identify defects and ensure proper functionality of applications and device.
  • User Acceptance Test (UAT): Execute User Acceptance Test on the production unit to ensure the product’s functionality performance prior shipment.
  • Bug Reporting: Document, track, and report defects or issues in the application and follow up on their resolution.
  • Test Planning: Assist in the creation of test protocol, test cases and test scenarios based on the requirements.
  • Collaboration: Work closely with developers, product managers, and other QA team members to ensure high-quality standards in product releases.
  • Regression Testing: Conduct regression testing to ensure that recent changes and updates to the product and ensure system integration do not affect its functionality and performance.
  • To support in customer complaint root cause investigation and issue verification related to software quality
  • Qualifications and Competencies: Bachelor's degree in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field.    1-3 years of experience in in software testing and technical documentation.
  • Core Competency: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to identify and troubleshoot complex product, system and application issues.
  • Proficiency in designing and executing manual test cases. Familiarity with SDLC and test management tools such as e.g. JIRA Self-starter, Effective communicator, and Team Player.     Proficiency in MS Excel, MS Word, SharePoint.
  • Able to work in a dynamic environment and handle multiple tasks at a time.

Good to Have:

  • Experience with automated testing & tools.
  • ISTQB certifications

postedCompany.description:
Prostate cancer is the 3rd most common cause of cancer amongst Singaporean men. Globally, approximately 5 million biopsies are performed annually and approximately 1 million men are diagnosed with prostate cancer.

The common method for prostate cancer diagnosis is a trans-rectal biopsy. As the rectum stores fecal matter, when biopsy punctures are made in the rectum wall, patients are susceptible to an infection rate of as high as 5%. This affects about 250,000 men globally. Costs of infection to the healthcare system is enormous due to re-admissions and follow up treatments, and deaths may occur if infections are not treated quickly.

Biobot Surgical’s iSR’obot Mona Lisa is a robotic platform that allows clinicians to mark out suspicious lesions using the proprietary planning software and imaging techniques fusing MRI and Ultrasound images. Subsequently, the robot takes over fully to position the needle guide to allow clinicians to fire off the biopsy needle to reach the intended target accurately via the perineum region located externally below the scrotum. Excellent coverage of the prostate is achieved with only 2 external skin punctures. The combination of robotic positioning and clear imaging techniques increase the chances of detecting early stage and aggressive cancer.
postedCompany.name: BIOBOT SURGICAL PTE. LTD.

districts.location: D05 Clementi New Town, Hong Leong Garden, Pasir Panjang
